content:
Canterbury Character
The Wife of Bath – A subversive, self-aware trickster who challenges dominant narratives.


✅ Why This Works:
• Ties literary themes to broader archetypal symbolism.
• Encourages students to think cross-disciplinarily (literature, astrology, mythology, psychology).
• Reinforces the role of storytelling structures through different traditions.

📜 3. Power Scaling Over the Course of the Semester

📜 Characters now thrive or struggle at different narrative points in the semester.
✔ Heroes are weak early, gain power in Act 2.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 167 (chunk 167/555)
